title: Writer Manifest (SQLite writes table)
description: Logical contract for the writer-manifest SQLite substrate (manifest.sqlite :: writes table) consumed by lib/manifest-record.sh (T-25) and the writer-reconciler (step 8.6). Basis is LangChain SQLRecordManager (denormalized append-only row insertion + logical supersession via row UPDATE rather than physical row delete + WAL journal mode for concurrent reader/writer safety + targeted indexes for the dominant audit query shapes). This schema validates the in-memory / on-the-wire object form (a top-level object with a writes[] array of row objects); the actual SQLite migration + CREATE TABLE DDL lives in T-25 outside this batch. The 12-field row contract is the public boundary that consumers validate against. Citations: writer-pipeline-layering.md L-96 (SQLRecordManager basis + WAL + index strategy) and L-109 (write_bucket partition enum). Authored under SP14 Batch D T-24 (2026-05-19).

writes.description: Array of writes-table row objects. Each row is an append-only insertion; supersession is logical (UPDATE of status + superseded_by on the predecessor row) rather than physical deletion.

id.description: UUID v4 or content-hash-based identifier; PRIMARY KEY surrogate for the writes table row.

writer_id.description: Writer identity — the writer-reference filename minus .md (e.g., meeting-processor, teams-scrape). Indexed for per-writer audit queries.

destination_path.description: Absolute path of the vault destination file written. Indexed for per-destination history queries (and the dominant 'who wrote this file?' audit query).

ingestion_date.description: ISO-8601 UTC timestamp of row insertion. Indexed for time-window audit queries and reconciler step 8.6 windowing.

source_id.description: Opaque writer-specific source identifier (e.g., Granola meeting ID, Teams message thread ID, Gmail thread ID). Optional — null for writers without an upstream source identity. Indexed for cross-source dedup + fan-in joins.

content_sha256.description: sha256 hex digest of the written body bytes. Used for idempotency check at reconciler step 8.6 (skip re-insertion if (writer_id, destination_path, content_sha256) tuple already present and active).

raw_path.description: Absolute path to the raw-retention file under $VAULT_WRITER_STATE_ROOT/raw/<writer-id>/ (per T-22 raw_retention block on vault-writer.md.json). Optional — null for writers that opt out of raw retention (e.g., direct-fetch connectors with durable upstream source).

status.description: Row lifecycle status. active = current row representing the live state of (writer_id, destination_path); superseded = predecessor row whose superseded_by points at the successor. Logical supersession per L-96 — no physical delete.

superseded_by.description: id of the writes-table row that superseded this row. Optional — null when status: active. Populated by UPDATE statement at amend time.

write_bucket.description: Partition discriminator for audit queries per §A62 / L-109. create = first write to the destination; modify-append = pure append (no LLM); modify-amend = LLM-driven amend round-trip through doc-amender + staging.

packet_kind.description: Source packet kind from staging-emit per L-106 / L-110 (packet_version 1.1+ admits all three; v1.0 packets pre-T-26 are recorded as null and treated as writer-emit by reconciler). Optional — null for v1.0-era rows.

notes.description: Free-form operator annotation. Optional — populated by /amend-accept or manual operator intervention. Not consumed by runtime.

_implementation_notes.description: SQLite implementation guidance (informational; not enforced by this schema). Journal mode: WAL (PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL) for concurrent reader/writer safety per L-96. Indexes (CREATE INDEX): one each on ingestion_date, destination_path, source_id, writer_id — covers the dominant audit query shapes (time-window scans, per-destination history, cross-source dedup, per-writer rollups). Insertion: append-only (INSERT). Supersession protocol: when a new row is inserted that supersedes a predecessor on the same (writer_id, destination_path), the predecessor row receives an UPDATE setting status='superseded' and superseded_by=<new row id>. No physical row deletion. Migration DDL + helper API surface live in T-25 (lib/manifest-record.sh) outside this batch.
